But, Gumayusi has proved his words once again and is now a Finals MVP on his three-peat winning night, being blessed by King ADC Uzi. His story itself is history.

In 2017, I was still a kid. Watching League of Legends matches made me feel very happy. That year and all of 2015 and 2016, I thought the Red Dynasty would continue its form until I saw Faker crying after losing. The moment Ruler shot Varus's ultimate at Faker made me choke up. SKT T1's attempt to win the 3-time consecutive championship in 2017 failed miserably. After watching the opening of this year, I remembered the feeling when I saw Faker fall at his home ground in 2017. The song Legends Never Die was like my obsession, painful and heartbreaking. And it was wonderful that history did not repeat itself. The Red Dynasty, which contained my youth, was once again burned and achieved what Faker failed to achieve in 2017. How wonderful.

This was PEAK gameplay, loved it so much and haven't even played league since S3
However watching these players do what they do always leaves me in awe, truly amazing and deserved World Champ title!

7:46:23 absolute cinema
7:49:31 the LOL himself bringing trophy

Everyone talks about how they don't understand how T1 always just sort of turns on in high pressure. They're athletes. They're exceptionally successful athletes. They LIVE for the pressure and LIVE for the competition. And there is no higher pressure and no higher competition than a game 5 of the World Finals or when the enemy team is at match point and the only way you win is to go Godmode. In those moments, that's where you see the "real" T1. Especially Faker. He's extremely expressive during games if you watch the little facial expressions when he plays. And the more high pressure the situation, the more "expressive" he gets. He lives for the 1v9. But he's grown enough as a player and is disciplined enough to not let his ego ruin it for everyone like you see with so many other legends of the sport.

What a Finals! GGs both teams! BDD, Cuzz & the boys deserve a lot of respect, they fought their hearts out (and I pray that they don't lose hope and give it another shot), but thank God T1 managed to reign it in and win for the 3-peat! I'm really proud of them.

Also, I'm so glad Gumayusi finally got the FMVP! He's been the rock in the botlane all these years. His Baron steals in 2022 and 2v1 vs Ruler in 2023 made me a fan. I'm happy he finally got the recognition he deserves!

Everyone had their moments, I wish I could give FMVPs to the others as well, especially Oner. Dude's such a solid Jungler. Still haven't forgotten that first-time Mundo in quarters! 😂
